questions.question: What is your full name?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My full name is {{full_name}}.
questions.guidance: State your full name exactly as it appears on your passport and official documents.
questions.tooltip: The officer verifies your identity against official records.

questions.question: What is your date of birth?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My date of birth is {{date_of_birth}}.
questions.guidance: Ensure your answer matches your passport and application forms exactly.
questions.tooltip: Used to confirm identity and eligibility under IR-2 age requirements.

questions.question: How old are you?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I am {{current_age}} years old.
questions.tooltip: Confirms eligibility as a qualifying child under immigration rules.

questions.question: Where were you born?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I was born in {{current_country}}.
questions.guidance: Provide both city and country clearly.
questions.tooltip: Ensures consistency with official birth records.

questions.question: Who is petitioning for you?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My {{sponsor_relationship}}, {{parent_full_name}}, is petitioning for me.
questions.guidance: Clearly mention both relationship and name.
questions.tooltip: Confirms the legal petitioner and relationship basis.

questions.question: What is your father's or mother's full name?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My {{sponsor_relationship}}'s full name is {{parent_full_name}}.
questions.guidance: State the full name exactly as it appears on official documents.
questions.tooltip: Used to verify the identity of the petitioner and match records.

questions.question: Is your parent a U.S. citizen or permanent resident?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My parent is a {{parent_citizenship_status}}.
questions.tooltip: Determines visa eligibility classification.

questions.question: Where does your parent live in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My parent lives in {{us_living_location}}.
questions.tooltip: Verifies petitioner's residence.

questions.question: What does your parent do for a living?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My parent has stable employment and is able to provide financial support.
questions.guidance: Keep your answer simple and factual.
questions.tooltip: Helps assess financial stability of the sponsor.

questions.question: How are you related to the petitioner?
questions.suggestedAnswer: The petitioner is my {{sponsor_relationship}}, and we share a genuine parent-child relationship.
questions.tooltip: Validates the legitimacy of the relationship.

questions.question: When was the last time you saw your parent?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I last saw my parent {{last_meeting_with_parent}}, and we have stayed in regular contact since then.
questions.guidance: Approximate timing is acceptable if exact date is unclear.
questions.tooltip: Evaluates ongoing relationship continuity.

questions.question: Do you talk to your parent regularly?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Yes, we communicate regularly through {{communication_method}}, which helps us stay close despite the distance.
questions.tooltip: Assesses emotional connection and relationship strength.

questions.question: Who raised you while growing up?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I was raised by my {{sponsor_relationship}} and other family members who have been responsible for my upbringing.
questions.guidance: Explain clearly, especially if different from petitioner.
questions.tooltip: Important for custody and parental responsibility verification.

questions.question: Do you have pictures with your parent?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{has_photos_with_parent ? 'Yes, we have photos together from different occasions that reflect our relationship.' : 'I have some photos with my parent that show our relationship.'}}
questions.tooltip: Supporting evidence of genuine relationship.

questions.question: Where will you live in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I will live with my parent in {{us_living_location}}, where they currently reside.
questions.tooltip: Confirms post-arrival residence plan.

questions.question: Who will you live with in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I will live with my {{sponsor_relationship}} and my family members there.
questions.tooltip: Ensures proper family support structure.

questions.question: How many people live in that household?
questions.suggestedAnswer: There are approximately {{household_size_us}} people living in the household.
questions.tooltip: Helps assess living arrangements and support.

questions.question: Do you know your home address in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Yes, I am aware of the address where I will be living with my parent in {{us_living_location}}.
questions.guidance: Knowing at least city and state is recommended. Exact address is a plus.
questions.tooltip: Shows preparedness and awareness of living arrangements.

questions.question: Do you have any siblings?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{has_siblings ? 'Yes, I have siblings, and we share a close family bond.' : 'No, I am an only child.'}}
questions.tooltip: Verifies family structure.

questions.question: Where do your siblings live?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My siblings live in {{siblings_location}}.
questions.guidance: Mention the location clearly. City or country is sufficient.
questions.tooltip: Tests familiarity with immediate family members' whereabouts.

questions.question: How often do you communicate with your siblings?
questions.suggestedAnswer: We maintain regular contact and stay connected as a family despite the distance.
questions.tooltip: Verifies consistency and strength of family relationships.

questions.question: Do you have any family members in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{living_with_in_us ? 'Yes, my family member is living in the United States.' : 'My primary family is outside the United States.'}}
questions.guidance: Answer accurately about family ties in the U.S.
questions.tooltip: Identifies existing family ties in the U.S.

questions.question: Why are you going to the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: I am going to the United States to live with my parent and be together as a family, which is very important to me.
questions.tooltip: Core purpose of IR-2 visa category.

questions.question: What will you do after arriving in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: After arriving, I plan to {{plans_after_arrival}} and live with my family.
questions.guidance: Keep your answer realistic and natural.
questions.tooltip: Evaluates future plans and stability.

questions.question: Do you plan to study in the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{plans_to_study ? 'Yes, I plan to continue my education and focus on my studies after moving.' : 'I will focus on adjusting to my new environment and family life.'}}
questions.tooltip: Education plans are expected for children.

questions.question: Are you excited to move to the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Yes, I am excited to move to the United States because I will be living with my parent and starting a new chapter of my life.
questions.guidance: Keep your answer natural and positive.
questions.tooltip: Assesses emotional readiness and genuine intent.

questions.question: Have you ever been to the United States before?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_us_visits ? 'Yes, I have visited before and followed all visa conditions.' : 'No, I have never been to the United States before.'}}
questions.tooltip: Checks past immigration compliance.

questions.question: Have you ever been denied a visa?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{previous_visa_refusal ? 'Yes, and the details have been disclosed honestly in my application.' : 'No, I have never been denied a visa.'}}
questions.tooltip: Past refusals are carefully reviewed.

questions.question: Do you have any criminal record?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{criminal_history ? 'Yes, and it has been disclosed truthfully.' : 'No, I do not have any criminal record.'}}
questions.tooltip: Security clearance requirement.

questions.question: When did your parent marry your step-parent?
questions.suggestedAnswer: My parent married my step-parent, and we have established a respectful family relationship.
questions.tooltip: Important for stepchild eligibility verification.

questions.question: Do you know your step-parent?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Yes, I know my step-parent and we have a respectful and positive relationship.
questions.guidance: Answer honestly. It's okay if the relationship is limited but be truthful.
questions.tooltip: Evaluates family integration in step-parent scenarios.

questions.question: Do you currently live with your biological parent?
questions.suggestedAnswer: {{lived_with_parent ? 'Yes, I currently live with my parent.' : 'No, but I maintain a relationship with my parent and we stay in regular contact.'}}
questions.guidance: Explain clearly if not living together.
questions.tooltip: Evaluates custody and relationship consistency.

questions.question: Does your other parent know that you are moving to the United States?
questions.suggestedAnswer: Yes, my other parent is aware of my move and there are no objections.
questions.tooltip: Prevents potential custody or legal issues.
